title: Flawed Left View Node Removal in Binary Tree

statement.markdown:
Given a binary tree, remove the nodes from the left view without disrupting the tree structure.

The left view of a tree is the set of nodes that appear when we look at the tree from the left side. The left view of a tree can be obtained by traversing the tree in a way that moves only right and goes as far as it can to the left.

Example:

```
   1        
  / \       
 2   3      
 \\       \\ 
 4   5      
``` 

The left view of the given tree is `[1, 2, 4]`. If we remove nodes `2`, `4`, and `5` from the left view, the tree should remain the same.

Write a function that removes nodes from the left view of a binary tree without disrupting the tree structure.
statement.inputFormat:
Input will be provided in the following format.
	Root Node Value
statement.outputFormat: Output should be the binary tree after removing nodes from the left view. If the tree is empty, return an empty list.
statement.notes: Time complexity should be O(n), where n is the number of nodes in the tree. Space complexity should be O(h), where h is the height of the tree.

examples.input: Example input (matching the stdin format that main wrapper expects)

solutions.python:
class Solution:
   def removeLeftViewNodes(self, root: TreeNode) -> TreeNode:
       if root is None: return None
       if root.left is None: return root
       root.left = self.removeLeftViewNodes(root.left)
       return root

   def inorder(self, root):
       if root is None: return
       self.inorder(root.left)
       print(root.val, end=' ')
       self.inorder(root.right)

   def printBinaryTree(self, root):
       self.inorder(root)


if __name__ == '__main__':
   root = TreeNode(1)
   root.left = TreeNode(2)
   root.right = TreeNode(3)
   root.left.left = TreeNode(4)
   root.right.right = TreeNode(5)
   solution = Solution()
   solution.printBinaryTree(solution.removeLeftViewNodes(root))
